assertion_results.text: Duplicate check searches for sibling issues using JQL with the CVE label and excludes the current issue key (Step 4)
assertion_results.evidence: duplicate-check.md line 5 shows the JQL query: "project = TC AND labels = 'CVE-2026-31812' AND issuetype = 10024 AND key != TC-8003" — this searches by CVE label and explicitly excludes the current issue key TC-8003.

assertion_results.text: Duplicate check correctly classifies TC-7999 as a same-stream sibling (both have stream suffix [rhtpa-2.2]) and identifies it as a duplicate (Step 4.1)
assertion_results.evidence: duplicate-check.md lines 15-18: "TC-8003 stream suffix: [rhtpa-2.2] --> stream 2.2.x" and "TC-7999 stream suffix: [rhtpa-2.2] --> stream 2.2.x" followed by "Both issues have the same stream suffix [rhtpa-2.2]. TC-7999 is therefore a same-stream sibling." Line 28 confirms duplicate identification: "the recommendation is to close the current issue (TC-8003) as Duplicate."

assertion_results.text: Triage outcome recommends closing TC-8003 as Duplicate with resolution 'Duplicate', referencing TC-7999 as the original (Step 4.1)
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md line 4: "TC-8003 should be closed as Duplicate of TC-7999." Line 49: "Transition TC-8003 to Closed with resolution 'Duplicate'." duplicate-check.md line 32: "Close TC-8003 as Duplicate of TC-7999."

assertion_results.text: The close recommendation includes a comment documenting why it is a duplicate — same CVE tracked for the same stream (Step 4.1)
assertion_results.evidence: duplicate-check.md line 38 proposes comment: "Duplicate of TC-7999 -- same CVE (CVE-2026-31812) tracked for the same stream [rhtpa-2.2]. Version impact analysis confirms overlap. TC-7999 is already In Progress with Affects Versions [RHTPA 2.2.0, RHTPA 2.2.1]." triage-outcome.md lines 47-48 repeat the same comment. Both explicitly state same CVE and same stream as the reason.

assertion_results.text: The analysis does NOT proceed to remediation task creation — duplicate detection short-circuits the triage flow
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md lines 37-43 explicitly lists skipped steps: Step 5 (Version Lifecycle Check) "Not needed", Step 6 (Already Fixed Check) "Not needed", Step 7 (Concurrent Triage Detection) "Not needed", Step 8 (Remediation) "Not needed -- TC-7999 owns remediation for this CVE in the 2.2.x stream." No remediation tasks are created anywhere in the outputs.
